message:
chore(beads): close franken_numpy-14rm (check_compliance_matrices.sh keep-going)

Routine status flip from in_progress to closed. The bug (the script
in commit 01e8f1f used a `set -e` for-loop that aborted on the first
failing generator, hiding regressions in the remaining two) has been
fixed in scripts/check_compliance_matrices.sh; all three generators
now run regardless of intermediate exit codes and the final exit
reflects ANY failure (gate or drift), satisfying the acceptance
criteria.

closed_at present, no close_reason field set on this bead. No source
changes here; only the ledger update.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -23,7 +23,7 @@
 {"id":"franken_numpy-0y0z","title":"Expose mgrid and ogrid in fnp-python","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-16T23:43:25.255854390Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-16T23:55:05.930398463Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T23:55:05.928368257Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-0zp4","title":"numpy.hamming python wrapper parity","description":"Add fnp_python.hamming passthrough wrapper around numpy.hamming(M). Returns the M-point Hamming window. Include oracle test covering: M=4 returning 4-element vector, M=11 (odd length), M=1 returning [1.0], M=0 returning empty, sum-near-M*0.54 sanity check, dtype float64, and a cross-check that hamming + hanning are siblings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:01:30.756643880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:50:16.073145743Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:50:16.071272513Z","close_reason":"Closed by cc-numpy — wrapper + oracle parity test delivered in commit 4ebccc8 (verified via git log --grep br-franken_numpy-0zp4).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-0zxx","title":"numpy.linalg.svdvals python wrapper parity","description":"Add fnp_python.svdvals passthrough wrapper around numpy.linalg.svdvals(x). Returns just the singular values. Include oracle test covering: square matrix returning sorted-desc singular values, rectangular tall matrix, rectangular wide matrix, identity matrix → all-ones, singular matrix → at least one zero singular value, batched 3-D input, and equivalence to svd(x)[1].","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T11:53:11.115750574Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:11:36.320126125Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:11:36.318276936Z","close_reason":"Closed by cc-numpy — wrapper fn svdvals and #[test] parity case svdvals_matches_numpy_across_shapes_and_batched_input are present in committed crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs at HEAD. Acceptance criteria (wrapper exists, oracle test committed) satisfied.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"franken_numpy-14rm","title":"[BUG] check_compliance_matrices.sh aborts on first generator failure","description":"scripts/check_compliance_matrices.sh (commit 01e8f1f) iterates the
three generators in a for-loop with `set -e`. If generator N fails
its own gate (e.g. MUST coverage < 95%), the loop aborts and the
remaining generators never run. Result: partial visibility into
matrix regressions when multiple subtrees drift in the same PR.

Self-review on commit 01e8f1f, found 2026-04-25.

Fix: collect generator exit codes + any drift in a flag variable,
keep running, then surface a combined failure at the end. Engineers
investigating a regression see the full surface picture in one run.

Acceptance:
- All 3 generators run regardless of intermediate exit codes.
- Final exit reflects ANY failure (gate or drift).
- The diff dump still shows per-output drift so the engineer can
  attribute regressions correctly.","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T19:54:41.350879568Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T19:55:01.702253265Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_numpy-14rm","title":"[BUG] check_compliance_matrices.sh aborts on first generator failure","description":"scripts/check_compliance_matrices.sh (commit 01e8f1f) iterates the
three generators in a for-loop with `set -e`. If generator N fails
its own gate (e.g. MUST coverage < 95%), the loop aborts and the
remaining generators never run. Result: partial visibility into
matrix regressions when multiple subtrees drift in the same PR.

Self-review on commit 01e8f1f, found 2026-04-25.

Fix: collect generator exit codes + any drift in a flag variable,
keep running, then surface a combined failure at the end. Engineers
investigating a regression see the full surface picture in one run.

Acceptance:
- All 3 generators run regardless of intermediate exit codes.
- Final exit reflects ANY failure (gate or drift).
- The diff dump still shows per-output drift so the engineer can
  attribute regressions correctly.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T19:54:41.350879568Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T19:55:59.619622992Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T19:55:59.617093742Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-1ck","title":"Run full workspace regression sweep on current shared tree","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:34:50.749939284Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T03:37:06.578912951Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T03:37:06.577867054Z","close_reason":"Full RCH workspace regression sweep passed on current shared tree.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-1ddt","title":"numpy.nanprod python wrapper parity","description":"Add fnp_python.nanprod passthrough wrapper around numpy.nanprod(a, axis=None, dtype=None, out=None, keepdims=<no value>, initial=<no value>, where=<no value>). Include oracle test covering: 1-D with NaN treated as multiplicative identity, 2-D axis=0/1 reductions, explicit dtype promotion, keepdims=True shape parity, all-NaN slice returning 1, and integer input matching numpy product semantics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:00:50.171646708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:34:13.902955838Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T16:34:13.901021377Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-1fic","title":"PORT random: RandomState legacy power parity","description":"Implement RandomState.power(a, size=None) with NumPy legacy MT19937 uniform-stream parity, scalar/shaped output, validation/no-advance errors, and live NumPy oracle coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:18:30.398924653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T19:24:42.648911130Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T19:24:42.647077781Z","close_reason":"Implemented RandomState.power legacy parity with stream-state edge cases and PyO3 NumPy oracle coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_numpy-1fic","depends_on_id":"franken_numpy-2h2s","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:18:55.968014531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
